perturbation.original_text: 1. Beijing SINA and SINA Leju agree that, upon the date of execution of this Termination Agreement, the Agreement shall terminate and be of no further force or effect, and, for the avoidance of doubt, no provisions of the Original Agreement survive such termination.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Beijing SINA and SINA Leju agree that, upon the date of execution of this Termination Agreement, the Agreement shall terminate and be of no further force or effect, except for the provisions outlined in Exhibit C, and, for the avoidance of doubt, provisions of the Original Agreement as described in Exhibit C survive such termination.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that the Original Agreement terminates except for provisions outlined in a new Exhibit C, while the original text states 'no provisions of the Original Agreement survive such termination.' This creates uncertainty about which parts of the original agreement remain valid after termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Beijing SINA and SINA Leju agree that, upon the date of execution of this Termination Agreement, the Agreement shall terminate and be of no further force or effect, except for the provisions outlined in Exhibit C, and, for the avoidance of doubt, provisions of the Original Agreement as described in Exhibit C survive such termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that certain provisions of the Original Agreement survive termination as outlined in Exhibit C, directly contradicting the original statement that 'no provisions of the Original Agreement survive such termination.' The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, highlighting that the very alteration introduces this in-text contradiction as it now conflicts with what was initially agreed upon, and with the unedited sections of the agreement. This inconsistency directly aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', creating uncertainty about the enforceable terms.

perturbation.original_text: 2. This Termination Agreement represents the complete, integrated, and entire agreement between the parties, and may not be modified except in writing signed by the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. This Termination Agreement represents the complete, integrated, and entire agreement between the parties with the exception of any verbal agreements made prior, and may be modified through verbal consent with a documented witness present.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction. Initially, the termination agreement represents the entire agreement between both parties and cannot be modified except in writing signed by both parties. The updated text now states that the agreement can be modified through verbal consent with a documented witness present. This directly contradicts the initial clause and introduces uncertainty about how the agreement can be modified.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. This Termination Agreement represents the complete, integrated, and entire agreement between the parties with the exception of any verbal agreements made prior, and may be modified through verbal consent with a documented witness present.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states the agreement can only be modified in writing signed by both parties. The changed text allows for verbal modifications with a documented witness, directly contradicting the prior requirement of written consent. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation definition, where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.
